81606.name: The Cargo Hold
81606.description: Hundreds of barrels containing food, water, rum and other looted necessities for the long periods between ports are all packed together in a large, dark, damp room.

81613.name: The Holding Cells
81613.description: You stand in a dimly lit room just aft of the cargo hold. Fish bones and other less savory things litter the ground. To starboard is some kind of holding cell.

81614.name: A Small Cell
81614.description: You now stand in the very bowels of the ship, where they hold captured foes until there's nothing left of them but broken shells. Looking around, you see what drives them to insanity. As the ocean pushes in from the outside, small drops of salt water manage to leak through the wood, hitting the floor with an uneven; drip, drip. Because of this, the floor nearest the hull has begun to rot away, adding it's own purveying stench to the already uncomfortable room.

81724.name: Before the Pirate Ship
81724.description: You made it to the edge of the pirate ship alive. Looking back you can see the bare outline of a coast. Turning your attention back to the ship, you notice a rope ladder lowered down the barnacle encrusted side.

81725.name: The Open Sea
81725.description: You are now close enough to the distant ship to be able to make out more detail. Your eyes are instantly drawn to the raised flag above the mast - the Jolly Roger. It's not too late to turn back now.

81726.name: The Open Sea
81726.description: The distant ship is still little more than a brown blob on the horizon, but it is a welcome change in the scenery. Waves crash around you and the ocean surrounds you on every other side.

81727.name: The Open Sea
81727.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. As you scan the surrounding waters, you see a small shape in the distant west.

81728.name: The Open Sea
81728.description: The distant ship does not appear to be coming any closer. Waves crash around you and the ocean surrounds you on all sides.

81605.name: Below Deck
81605.description: Rows upon rows of bunks hang from the ceiling here, creaking gently as the winds drifts through the room. Above it all is a stench that comes from a few score men being at sea for months at a time.

81609.name: The Galley
81609.description: There isn't a clean dish in sight or anything that looks even distinctly edible. This is clearly not where the officers eat. A few tables and empty barrels, for sitting, line the walls.

81611.name: A Dim Hallway
81611.description: The lanterns in this room are out and only vague light flickers in from the connected rooms causing uneasy shadows to dance along the walls.

81616.name: The Kitchen
81616.description: Everything here looks simply...delicious. Week old fish are hanging up to dry, the remains of something vaguely resembling a squid lays on the counter and a pot of gruel sits boiling over a fire.

81617.name: Officer's Mess
81617.description: A clear step up from the sorry state of the regular galley, this room is for when the officers come to eat. Chairs and tables are set up around the room and there's a direct window to the kitchen complete with a calling bell.

81618.name: A Dark Hallway
81618.description: Proceeding down this hallway, you notice that the only source of light comes from a single waning torch bracketed to the wall. Each step sends a shiver down your spine as the aged planks creak and groan.

81619.name: Port Slave Galley
81619.description: Bolted to the port wall of the ship are dozens of chains and an equal number of oars. The room is abustle with the rustle of iron, the straining of wood and the crack of a whip.

81620.name: A Dark Hallway
81620.description: Proceeding down this hallway, you notice that the only source of light comes from a single waning torch bracketed to the wall. Each step sends a shiver down your spine as the aged planks creak and groan.

81621.name: Starboard Slave Galley
81621.description: Bolted to the starboard wall of the ship are dozens of chains and an equal number of oars. The room is abustle with the rustle of iron, the straining of wood and the crack of a whip.

81603.name: Port Side
81603.description: Looking around you, you take in the whole pirate ship. Directly starboard is the mast, forward along the ship you see the helm on a slight rise and over the port side railing, the ocean stretches as far as you can see.

81604.name: Beneath the Jolly Roger
81604.description: You are standing before a great oaken mast, bringing the mind the towering trees of the gnomish homeland. High above flies the Jolly Roger, waving ominously.

81622.name: Forward Port Side
81622.description: The hull as a slight curve to it as it approaches the bow. Looking over the railing, there is ocean for as far as the eye can see.

81623.name: The Bow
81623.description: You now stand at the bow of the ship. Looking forward you are momentarily distracted by the beauty created by the light-dappled water. Returning to your surroundings, you notice the ships figurehead just below you and some stairs leading to a slightly raised platform behind you.

81624.name: Forward Starboard Side
81624.description: The hull has a slight curve to it as it approaches the bow. Looking over the railing, there is ocean for as far as the eye can see.

81625.name: Starboard Side
81625.description: To your left a great oaken mast raises from the deck of the ship like a massive tree. Looking over the railing, there is ocean for as far as the eye can see.

81626.name: Aft Starboard Side
81626.description: The hull curves port side as it approaches the stern. Looking over the railing, there is ocean for as far as the eye can see.

81627.name: The Stern
81627.description: Taking a long look at the stern, you notice the masterful craftsmanship that went into the ship. Sweeping rails come in from the port and starboard sides, tapering off into a grand balcony overlooking the ocean. Who could of thought such beauty and majesty could take place on a pirate ship.

81628.name: Aft Port Side
81628.description: The hull curves starboard side as it approaches the stern. Looking over the railing, you see the ocean far below.

81631.name: Crow's Nest
81631.description: You stand near the top of the oaken mast, towering high above the ship. Looking to the west, you see the J'rel boat, other than that all around you are calm waters, if not for the chaos far below, it might actually be a relaxing sight. A little ways above you flies the Jolly Roger.

81629.name: The Helm
81629.description: You stand on a slightly raised platform near the front of the ship. Situated in the center is the steering wheel.

81630.name: Climbing the Mast
81630.description: Looking down, you've noticed that you have climbed to dizzying heights. A fall from here would not be good. A little ways above you sits the crow's nest and salvation.

81632.name: Before the Jolly Roger
81632.description: Before you, flapping in the wind, is the very emblem of pirates everywhere. It is attached to the mast through a series of ropes which look to have begun fraying.
